results.title: Varsity blues
results.creator_credit: Brian Robbins, Mike Tollin, Tova Laiter and Paramount Pictures
results.brief_description: Eager to prove he is more than just another soap star, James Van Der Beek agreed to the title role of Jonathan “Mox” Moxton in this coming of age film set in Texas. Mox is a reluctant gridiron fullback in the West Canaan Coyotes who discovers his brilliance for the game after being called in to substitute for injured star player Lance Harbor (Paul Walker). Mox is not thrilled at being pushed into the limelight as it means undue attention from masochistic Coach Kilmer (Jon Voight) - eager to win the 23rd district trophy, increased pressure from his father who is using Mox to live out his own unfulfilled sporting aspirations, and grief from his girlfriend who says that Mox is deluding himself by being a football hero. Things come to a head on the eve of the final when Mox is blackmailed by Coach Kilmer into play the game “his way” or face the prospect of remaining in their one horse town when he sabotages Mox’s chance at taking up his university offer. Cast also includes Ron Lester, Scott Caan, Ali Larter and Amy Smart.

results.title: The Tenant of Wildfell Hall
results.creator_credit: Mike Barker, Suzan Harrison, BBC, Canadian Broadcasting Corporation and WGBH (Boston)
results.brief_description: Anne Bronte’s novel of an unhappy marriage deals with the themes of self-sacrifice, adulterous passion and redemption set amongst the rugged but beautiful landscape of the Yorkshire Dales. Helen Graham, a widow with a young son, is the new tenant of Wildfell Hall. Her radical views and her aloofness attracts the local young squire Gilbert Markham but despite the attraction, Helen has a secret which prevents their love from blossoming. Starring Tara Fitzgerald, Toby Stephens and Rupert Graves.

results.title: Moonlighting
results.creator_credit: Jerzy Skolimowski, Mark Shivas, Centar Film and Michael White
results.brief_description: Three Polish builders and their foreman, Nowak, (Jeremy Irons), the only one to speak English, enter Britain illegally to renovate the home of a rich fellow Pole. All goes according to plan, but it is December 1981, time of the military crackdown in Poland and Nowak, the only one to find out, decides not to tell the others. Things start to go badly wrong, the workers are restless, money begins to run out, and Nowak resorts to increasingly desperate measures to keep everything under control. A Cannes Film Festival award winner.

results.title: Instinct
results.creator_credit: Jon Turteltaub, Barbara Boyle, Brian Doubleday, Christina Steinberg, Michael Taylor, Richard Lerner and Touchstone Pictures
results.brief_description: This psychological thriller is about an anthropologist, Ethan Powell, who leaves human society to live among gorillas. Fully ensconced in this animal world, Powell is extradited back to the United States after murdering two gorilla poachers. Once there, it falls to an ambitious psychiatrist to discover the non-communicative Powell’s secrets. With in-form performances by Anthony Hopkins and Cuba Gooding Jr, this film attempts with profundity to address human nature, harmony with the natural world, and what motivates passions bordering on insanity. Cast also includes Donald Sutherland.

results.title: Gore Vidal's Myra Breckinridge
results.creator_credit: Michael Sarne, Robert Fryer and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Based on the novel by Gore Vidal and directed by Michael Sarne, this film adaptation is a highly stylised and over acted parody of the Hollywood system and a surreptitious study of gender and sexual identity. Myron (Rex Reed) undergoes a sex change to become the woman he has always felt inside - Myra (Raquel Welch). Myra arrives at her Uncle Buck’s (John Huston) acting school claiming to be Myron’s widow and arguing for her half-share of the business. Uncle Buck lets her teach the weird and wonderful ‘students’ of the school while he investigates her claim with lawyers. Mae West features as an agent for leading men (“Get your resumes out boys”) resplendent in Edith Head designed costumes and satirising herself as part of the very loose plot. An absurdist take on one man’s life and sexual identity intercut throughout with some memorable and not-so memorable clips of classic Hollywood feature films and stars. Musical numbers, hippies and homosexual men get Myra though her day until it all becomes too much and her inner Myron has an run-in with a car. Cast includes Farrah Fawcett and Tom Selleck.

results.title: The Mango tree
results.creator_credit: Kevin Dobson, Michael Pate and Pisces Productions
results.brief_description: Jamie’s life is touched by all the experiences around him; he sees how a seemingly hopeless drunk can be a man of wisdom and an unforgettable friend; how a sadistic teacher can vent all his pent-up hatred on one unfortunate boy; how a “man of God”, trapped by his own sense of righteousness can turn brutally and commit murder; how people can join forces in times of utmost need, or turn against that which they don’t know or understand. Based on the novel by Ronald McKie. Stars Christopher Pate, Geraldine Fitzgerald, Robert Helpmann and Gerard Kennedy.

results.title: The Little rascals
results.creator_credit: Penelope Spheeris, Bill Oakes, Gerald Molen, Michael King, King World and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: Spanky, Alfalfa, and the other ‘little rascals’ made famous in the Hal Roach ‘Our Gang’ shorts of the 1920s and 1930s, test the boundaries of friendship when Alfalfa falls head over heels for Darla. As a member of the “He-Man Womun Haters Club’, Alfalfa has sworn to hate women, and must hide his crush. A task he believes will be simple, as the boys are focused on the upcoming go-cart derby, and their prize-winning go-cart ‘The Blur’. Unfortunately Alfalfa’s best friend and club president Spanky discovers his friend has breached the number one club rule, and attempts to sabotage the relationship before it begins, starting a chain reaction of comical mistakes and misunderstandings. Features cameos by Whoopi Goldberg, Donald Trump, Mel Brooks, Daryl Hannah and Reba McEntire.

results.title: French kiss
results.creator_credit: Lawrence Kasdan, Eric Fellner, Kathryn F. Galan, Meg Ryan, Tim Bevan, PolyGram Filmed Entertainment, Prufrock Pictures, Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation and Working Title Films
results.brief_description: Kevin Kline and Meg Ryan star in this romantic comedy about two people stuck in Paris, France. The most romantic city in the world. A place where anything can happen. And it does!
results.creator_credit_for_label: Lawrence Kasdan, Eric Fellner, Kathryn F. Galan, Meg Ryan, Tim Bevan, PolyGram Filmed Entertainment, Prufrock Pictures, Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation and Working Title Films

results.title: The Unsinkable Molly Brown
results.creator_credit: Helen Deutsch, Lawrence Weingarten and Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer
results.brief_description: Debbie Reynolds stars in her Oscar nominated performance as the eponymous Molly Brown. Destined for better things, Western orphan Molly leaves her mountain cabin and her uneducated and poor upbringing in search of a rich husband and a better life in Denver society. In a typical rags to riches fashion, Molly becomes the wealthiest woman in society. Based on the musical play with music and lyrics by Meredith Wilson. Screen play by Helen Deutsch. Cast includes Debbie Reynolds, Harve Presnell and Ed Begley.

results.title: The Patriot [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Roland Emmerich, Dean Devlin, Gary Levinsohn, Mark Gordon, Centropolis Entertainment, Columbia Pictures and Mutual Film Company
results.brief_description: A historical drama set during the American Revolution, based on the true story of war hero, General Francis Marion. Following the death of his wife, Colonel Benjamin Martin is content to abandon his post in the army to raise his seven children on his plantation in South Carolina. Turning his back on violence, Benjamin is stunned when his oldest son, Gabriel, leaves home and joins the fight against the British Army, despite his father’s refusal to join the Continental Army. Gabriel is injured and makes his way home to warn his family the war is getting closer. His warning is too late however, and when the English arrive, a fight breaks out on Benjamin’s plantation. When Colonel Tavington kills his son, Thomas, and captures Gabriel, Benjamin rethinks his decision not to fight. Vowing to avenge his son’s death, he rescues Gabriel and together they form a militia group, made up of Carolina patriots who will do anything to stop the British troops, and their leader Lord General Cornwallis. Cast includes Mel Gibson, Heath Ledger, Joely Richardson, Jason Isaacs and Tom Wilkinson.
